description:
fixtures/hello.fz exercises `print(:ok)` and `print(nil)`. Captured expected output (fz-ul4.23.1 bless):

  42
  :atom_1
  true
  []

`:atom_1` should be `:ok` — the JIT's print path is rendering atoms via the synthetic `atom_N` name (likely an atom-interner display, not the source name lookup). `[]` should be `nil` — the print path is collapsing the nil tag into the empty-list representation.

## Acceptance

- `print(:ok)` outputs `:ok`
- `print(nil)` outputs `nil` (or whichever spelling the user wants — needs a one-line decision; current behavior is "empty list" which is wrong for an atom-like nil)
- fixtures/hello.expected re-blessed; matrix green

## Where to look

Print is wired through ir_codegen's `print` builtin FFI into runtime / value display. Atom name resolution probably wants to consult the SAME atom table that the source uses; today print is using a synthetic name path.
title: print() renders atoms by interner id and nil as []
